tweets.text:
A judge issued a final removal order for Cuban criminal alien Odalys Abreu in 1995, when she was in her early twenties. 

Our officers arrested her in Tampa, Fla., as a criminal illegal alien with:
• Drug and larceny related charges 
• Unlawful reentry into the U.S.
• Laken Riley Act violations

➡️The Laken Riley Act requires the detention of aliens charged with or convicted of a theft crime, crimes that result in serious injury or death, or assaulting an officer.
tweets.text_resolved:

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
In non-sanctuary jurisdictions, we make our immigration arrests securely in the jail or prison that already has the criminal alien in custody.

In sanctuary jurisdictions, we’re forced to make our arrests in public and that requires more officers and agents — often, an entire federal team. 

In other words, sanctuary policies are a waste of taxpayer money – and a risk to the safety of our brave officers, as well as innocent bystanders and the criminals themselves.
tweets.text_resolved:
In non-sanctuary jurisdictions, we make our immigration arrests securely in the jail or prison that already has the criminal alien in custody.

In other words, sanctuary policies are a waste of taxpayer money – and a risk to the safety of our brave officers, as well as innocent bystanders and the criminals themselves.

tweets.text:
Chinese criminal alien Shenghua Wen will serve 96 months thanks to an investigation by ICE Homeland Security Investigations and our federal partners. 

He conspired to export sensitive technology, ammunition and firearms into North Korea at North Korean officials’ direction.  

He entered the U.S. on a student visa in 2012 and remained in the U.S. illegally after it expired the following year. 

➡️Learn more on the DOJ’s press release webpage.

tweets.text:
Cruz Miguel Aguina repeatedly raped his biological daughter over the course of a year but denied past crimes when he was naturalizing.  

Later, he was charged, convicted and sentenced to 29 years in prison. 

Our ICE Operation False Haven investigation led to a naturalization fraud conviction and denaturalization. He’ll face deportation when he gets out of prison. 

💻 Related court records are available on PACER by searching for case nos. 5:22-cr-00253-1BO-RJ.

tweets.text:
The U.S. Attorney’s Office for the Western District of Washington indicted eight people this week on charges of conspiracy to distribute fentanyl. Five others were arrested on other firearms and narcotics-related charges.

Many of the defendants are members of the Knoccout Crips street gang tied to drug trafficking and violence in Tacoma.

The arrests followed an 18-month investigation where we proudly assisted the @FBI.

tweets.text:
A previously convicted child sex offender, Alexander James Koby of Jacksonville, Fla., pleaded guilty Aug. 20 to receiving child sexual abuse material over the internet.

ICE seized three devices, and after forensic examination, discovered 2,311 photos and 103 videos of kids being sexually abused.

Koby was convicted of a similar CSAM offense in 2014 and was released from prison in 2019 to serve a 25-year term of supervised release.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Thanks to an investigation by ICE special agents, Michigan resident Mattek Scott pleaded guilty Aug. 21 to conspiracy to commit wire fraud for his role in a multistate retail theft scheme.

He and his co-conspirator Ariana Asberry fraudulently obtained prepaid gift cards, money and merchandise from a Rhode Island-based business at multiple locations across 11 states.

The value of their theft is estimated at about $140K.
tweets.text_resolved:
